facts.statement: The SEC submissions metadata identifies the registrant as GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC, with ticker GS, listed on NYSE, and SIC description "Security Brokers, Dealers & Flotation Companies."
Goldman Sachs' proxy describes execution of strategic objectives across Global Banking & Markets and Asset & Wealth Management.

The proxy lists Goldman Sachs' strategic objectives as: "Harness One Goldman Sachs to Serve Our Clients with Excellence," "Run World-Class, Differentiated, Durable Businesses," and "Invest to Operate at Scale."
For the year ended December 31, 2025, Goldman Sachs reported net revenues of $58.283 billion.

For the year ended December 31, 2025, Goldman Sachs reported pre-tax earnings of $21.852 billion, net earnings of $17.176 billion, net earnings applicable to common shareholders of $16.300 billion, and EPS of $51.32.
For the year ended December 31, 2025, Goldman Sachs reported operating expenses of $37.544 billion.
For 2025, Goldman Sachs reported ROE of 15.0% based on average common shareholders' equity of $108.726 billion.

The proxy states that ROTE is calculated by dividing net earnings applicable to common shareholders by average monthly tangible common shareholders' equity, and that tangible common shareholders' equity equals total shareholders' equity less preferred stock, goodwill, and identifiable intangible assets.

For the year ended December 31, 2025, Goldman Sachs' average total shareholders' equity was $123.733 billion, including $15.007 billion of preferred stock; average tangible common shareholders' equity was $101.954 billion after deducting goodwill of $5.915 billion and identifiable intangible assets of $857 million.
The SEC company facts summary reports cash and equivalents of $182.092 billion for FY2025, filed February 25, 2026.
The SEC company facts summary reports stockholders' equity of $116.905 billion for FY2025, filed February 25, 2026.
The SEC company facts summary reports operating cash flow of negative $12.587 billion for FY2025, filed February 25, 2026.

In the fourth quarter of 2025, Goldman Sachs entered into an agreement to transition the Apple Card program to another issuer; the proxy says results reflect the impact of transferring the credit card portfolio to held-for-sale status and related impacts of contract termination obligations and other expenses.

The 10-K states that installment loan balances decreased due to the sales of GreenSky and the seller financing loan portfolio in 2024, and that remaining installment loans originated by the firm were included in other loans beginning in the first quarter of 2025.

Goldman Sachs reported average interest-bearing deposits of $466.888 billion for 2025, compared with $428.934 billion for 2024; the average interest rate on total deposits was 3.94% in 2025 and 4.73% in 2024.
As of December 2025, Goldman Sachs reported uninsured deposits in U.S. offices of $120.30 billion and uninsured deposits in non-U.S. offices of $79.79 billion.

As of December 2025, Goldman Sachs reported gross loans of $239.882 billion, including $98.999 billion of other collateralized loans, $37.409 billion of commercial real estate loans, $31.957 billion of residential real estate loans, $30.676 billion of corporate loans, and $19.742 billion of credit card loans.

As of December 2025, Goldman Sachs' gross loans included $87.193 billion with tenor of one year or less, $8.853 billion of fixed-rate loans with tenor over one year, and $143.836 billion of floating-rate loans with tenor over one year.

Goldman Sachs' allowance for loan losses was $2.148 billion as of December 2025, compared with $4.666 billion as of December 2024; the credit card allowance was zero as of December 2025 versus $2.567 billion as of December 2024.

For 2025, Goldman Sachs reported total net charge-offs of $1.250 billion on average gross loans accounted for at amortized cost of $211.520 billion, for a 0.6% total net charge-off ratio; the consumer net charge-off ratio was 6.1% and the wholesale net charge-off ratio was 0.0%.

The 2025 Form 10-K signatures identify David Solomon as Director, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer; Denis P. Coleman III as Chief Financial Officer; and Sheara J. Fredman as Chief Accounting Officer.
The 2026 proxy lists 13 director nominees for election and states that Lakshmi Mittal will retire from the Board at the 2026 Annual Meeting after nearly 18 years of service.

Goldman Sachs' Lead Director letter states that the Board and its committees held 71 meetings in 2025; the Lead Director personally held over 125 additional meetings and engagements, and other committee chairs collectively had over 200 additional meetings and engagements.

The Lead Director letter states that, over the prior year, the Lead Director engaged with shareholders representing over 35% of shares outstanding, often alongside Compensation Committee Chair Kimberley Harris.

As of December 31, 2025, Goldman Sachs had 15,420,097 shares issuable pursuant to outstanding RSUs, 53,871,082 shares available for future issuance under equity compensation plans, no outstanding options, and no equity compensation plans not approved by security holders.

Goldman Sachs disclosed that its CEO and CFO concluded disclosure controls and procedures were effective as of the end of 2025; it also disclosed that during the quarter ended December 2025, no directors or executive officers entered into, modified, or terminated Rule 10b5-1 trading plans or non-Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangements.
